{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11590298]] Wikidata name: Shinmeiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shinmeiyama Kofun | native_name =神明山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shinmeiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tamba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline}} *[https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/694/ Kyōtango Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3302.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11590298]] Wikidata name: Shinmeiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|coordinates = {{Coord|35.735|N|135.111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shinmeiyama Kofun | native_name =神明山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shinmeiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shinmeiyama Kofun]][[en:Shinmeiyama Kofun]][[ja:神明山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11590298]] Wikidata name: Shinmeiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|coordinates = {{Coord|35.735|N|135.111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shinmeiyama Kofun | native_name =神明山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shinmeiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline}} *[https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/694/ Kyōtango Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3302.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shinmeiyama Kofun]][[en:Shinmeiyama Kofun]][[ja:神明山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590298]] Wikidata name: Shinmeiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Historic {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|coordinates = {{Coord|35.735|N|135.111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shinmeiyama Kofun | native_name =神明山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shinmeiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]

During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>

However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]

color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by [[:en:Doshisha University|Doshisha University]] fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the [[:en:Tango Province|Tango]] region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the [[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba]] region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], a [[:en:Sutra mound|Sutra mound]] was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by {{ill|Doshisha University|en|Doshisha University|WD=Q921088|lt=Doshisha University}} fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango}} region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba}} region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, a {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}} was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by {{ill|Doshisha University|en|Doshisha University|WD=Q921088|lt=Doshisha University}} fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango}} region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba}} region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, a {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound}} was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by {{ill|Doshisha University|en|Doshisha University|WD=Q921088|lt=Doshisha University|1=Doshisha University}} fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango|1=Tango Province}} region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}} region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, a {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}} was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}

The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by {{ill|Doshisha University|en|Doshisha University|WD=Q921088|lt=Doshisha University|1=Doshisha University}} fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango|1=Tango Province}} region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}} region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, a {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}} was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Historic {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.735|N|135.111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Shinmeiyama Kofun | native_name =神明山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shinmeiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shinmeiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|6.63|N|135|06|40.47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shinmeiyama Kofun'''|神明山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1923.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1646 |title=神明山古墳 |trans-title= Shinmeiyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shinmeiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The tumulus was constructed by cutting away the tip of a hill, and is orientated to the northeast. It was surveyed by {{ill|Doshisha University|en|Doshisha University|WD=Q921088|lt=Doshisha University|1=Doshisha University}} fringe 1964 to 1967, but no [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] has been conducted. The tumulus has a length 190 meters, making it one of the largest on the Sea of Japan coast. The surface of the mound was covered in white ''[[fukiishi]]'', and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Among the ''haniwa'', fragments of a type specific to the {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango|1=Tango Province}} region have been found, including one with a line engraving of a person rowing a boat. The details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear, but it is presumed to be a pit-type stone chamber based on the stone slabs that were scattered in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. During the Kofun period, this region of northern Kyoto prefecture had trade contacts with the Asian mainland, and the area around the mouth of the Takeno River was the center of an ancient kingdom. The tumulus was situated such that its white ''fukiishi'' would be visible as a landmark for any ships approaching the harbor. However, after the middle of the 5th century, trade decreased and the political center of the region shifted inland to the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba|1=Tanba Province}} region.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
During the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, a {{ill|Sutra mound|en|Sutra mound|WD=Q7650105|lt=Sutra mound|1=Sutra mound}} was established on the summit of this tumulus. Artifacts recovered from this sutra mound, including sutra containers and two {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyōtango City.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 190 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 78 meters wide x 15 meters high, 3-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 129 meter diameter x 26 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> 神明山古墳.jpg|Posterior Circular Portion of the tumulus 神明山古墳出土 舟と人物の線刻のある埴輪.JPG|Fragment of ''haniwa'' with engraving of man on a boat 神明山経塚出土 銅製経筒.JPG|burial goods recovered from sutra mound </gallery>
